examples.title: Insert Node at Beginning
examples.description: Function to insert a new node at the beginning of a singly linked list
examples.explanation: This function creates a new node, sets its next pointer to the current head, then updates head to point to the new node. This effectively adds the new node as the first element.

examples.title: Delete Node by Value
examples.description: Function to delete the first occurrence of a given value from singly linked list
examples.explanation: Three cases handled: (1) Empty list - return NULL, (2) Delete head - move head to next, (3) Delete other - bypass the node by updating previous->next. The key is preserving access to previous node.

examples.title: Display/ Traverse Linked List
examples.description: Function to traverse and display all elements in a singly linked list
examples.explanation: The traversal uses a current pointer that starts at head and moves to next until NULL is reached. Each node's data is printed before moving to the next. The loop condition checks for NULL to prevent infinite loops.
